target.name: Physical Intelligence
target.founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Physical Intelligence, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, and Brian Ichter, is valued at $5.6B post-money following $600M+ in total capital raised. The peer universe consists of 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Safe Superintelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the current $5.6B post-money valuation, implying $10.6B. Peer-Median analysis examines 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $500M to $32.0B; the median of this cohort is $3.0B. The wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in the AI & Software Series B market.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $2.5B (low) to $12.2B (high), with a median of $6.8B. The current $5.6B valuation sits within this range, slightly below the median. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x stage multiplier in a volatile AI market and the relevance of peer comparables given the extreme range observed ($500M to $32.0B)—particularly the outsized valuations of Safe Superintelligence relative to other Series B benchmarks. Revenue, capital efficiency, and technology differentiation data would sharpen this analysis.
